target.name: Alef Aeronautics
target.founder: Jim Dukhovny, Constantine Kisly, Pavel Markin, Oleg Petrov

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: REGENT Craft
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series A), post-money $270M

data.narrative:
Alef Aeronautics is a Series A-stage transportation company that has raised $30M+ to date. The relevant peer universe in Transportation comprises 8 private companies at various stages, including REGENT Craft, Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, and Harbinger. No public comparables exist in this narrow segment, limiting the applicability of traditional public-market benchmarking.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method, using the $30M total raised as a proxy for current value. Applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $60M. Peer-median analysis proved inapplicable; while five same-sector peers exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier companies yielded fewer than three datapoints, making a reliable median calculation unfeasible. A broader sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the heterogeneity of transportation subsectors.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $51M to $69M, with a median point of $60M. This range reflects standard step-up progression mechanics rather than corroborated peer trading or funding multiples. Key uncertainties include (1) the absence of sufficiently comparable Series A stage companies in transportation, (2) the heterogeneity of business models within the peer set, and (3) reliance on a single triangulation method. Additional visibility into comparable Series A funding rounds or near-term financing activity would materially strengthen conviction in this range.
